controls.label: Does your business have a Verification Standards — Documents process?
controls.remediation: Your organisation does not have a Verification Standards — Documents process in place. You must establish a documented Verification Standards — Documents process to meet your AML/CTF obligations. Review the controls below and implement the required procedures before completing this assessment.

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program include verification of KYC information about individuals by means of reliable and independent documentation?
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must include a documented approach to verifying KYC information about individuals using reliable and independent documentation. Define which documents your entity will accept for identity verification purposes, as required under rule 4.9.1.

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program require verification that documents used for KYC information have not expired (with the exception of Commonwealth passports expired within the preceding two years)?
controls.detail-label: Describe how you check document expiry during verification
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must ensure that all documents used to verify KYC information are checked for expiry before acceptance. Implement a control to reject expired documents, except for Commonwealth passports that expired within the preceding two years, as required under rule 4.9.2.

controls.label: Have you documented what reliable and independent documentation you will require to verify an individual's name, date of birth, and/or residential address?
controls.detail-label: Specify which documents you require for name, DOB, and residential address verification
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must specify in advance what reliable and independent documents you will accept to verify each core element of an individual's identity: name, date of birth, and residential address. Document your entity's policy on acceptable documents for each element, as required under rule 4.9.3(1).

controls.label: If you verify other KYC information about individuals beyond name, DOB, and address, have you documented what reliable and independent documentation may be used?
controls.detail-label: Describe what additional KYC information you verify and what documents you accept
controls.remediation: If your Verification of Identity procedure includes verification of additional KYC information beyond name, date of birth, and residential address, you must document what reliable and independent documents will be accepted for verification of each additional element, as required under rule 4.9.3(2).

controls.label: Have you established a risk-based policy on whether, and in what circumstances, you will accept copies of reliable and independent documents for verification of individual customer information?
controls.detail-label: Explain your policy on accepting certified copies, scans, or other copies
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must include a documented risk-based policy that clearly states whether you will accept copies of documents (such as certified copies or scans) for verifying individual customer information, and under what specific circumstances copies are acceptable. This policy must be recorded in your AML/CTF program, as required under rule 4.9.3(3).

controls.label: Have you established criteria and procedures to determine when you will take steps to verify whether an individual's document may have been forged, tampered with, cancelled, or stolen?
controls.detail-label: Describe your fraud detection procedures and what triggers verification steps
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must document the circumstances in which you will investigate whether an individual's identity document may have been forged, tampered with, cancelled, or stolen, and what specific steps you will take to establish the authenticity of the document. This risk-based approach is required under rule 4.9.3(4).

controls.label: Have you determined whether you will use any available authentication services or systems to verify the authenticity of individual customer documents?
controls.detail-label: List any authentication services you use (e.g., government verification systems, issuer checks)
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must address whether you will use any available authentication services (such as document issuer verification systems, government databases, or third-party authentication services) to verify documents provided by individuals. Document your decision on the use of authentication services in your AML/CTF program, as required under rule 4.9.3(5).

controls.label: Have you established a procedure to determine whether, and how, you will confirm KYC information about an individual by independently initiating contact with the person they claim to be?
controls.detail-label: Describe your process for independently verifying customer identity through direct contact
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must include a policy on whether you will conduct independent verification by directly contacting the individual to confirm their identity details, and if so, under what circumstances and through what methods. Document this approach in your AML/CTF program, as required under rule 4.9.3(6).

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program include verification of KYC information about customers who are not individuals (e.g., companies, trusts, partnerships) by means of reliable and independent documentation?
controls.detail-label: Describe your document-based verification procedure for non-individual customers
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must include a documented approach to verifying KYC information about non-individual customers (such as companies, trusts, or partnerships) using reliable and independent documentation. Define which documents your entity will accept for verifying non-individual customer information, as required under rule 4.9.4.

controls.label: Have you documented what reliable and independent documents you will use, and how many, for verifying non-individual customers?
controls.detail-label: Specify the number and types of documents required for non-individual customer verification
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must specify in advance what reliable and independent documents you will require to verify information about non-individual customers, including how many documents are required and which types are acceptable for different categories of entities. Document this policy in your AML/CTF program, as required under rule 4.9.5(1).

controls.label: Have you established risk-based criteria to determine whether a document is sufficiently contemporaneous (recent) for use in verifying non-individual customer information?
controls.detail-label: Describe how you determine if a document is current enough for verification purposes
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must document your risk-based approach to assessing the contemporaneousness (currency) of documents used to verify non-individual customer information. Define time thresholds or other criteria that determine whether a document is sufficiently recent for verification, as required under rule 4.9.5(2).

controls.label: Have you established a risk-based policy on whether, and in what circumstances, you will accept copies of reliable and independent documents for verification of non-individual customer information?
controls.detail-label: Explain your policy on accepting certified copies, scans, or other copies for non-individual customers
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must include a documented risk-based policy that clearly states whether you will accept copies of documents (such as certified copies or scans) for verifying non-individual customer information, and under what specific circumstances copies are acceptable. This policy must be recorded in your AML/CTF program, as required under rule 4.9.5(3).

controls.label: Have you established criteria and procedures to determine when you will take steps to verify whether a non-individual customer's document may have been cancelled, forged, tampered with, or stolen?
controls.detail-label: Describe your fraud detection procedures for non-individual customer documents
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must document the circumstances in which you will investigate whether a non-individual customer's document may have been cancelled, forged, tampered with, or stolen, and what specific steps you will take to establish the authenticity of the document. This risk-based approach is required under rule 4.9.5(4).

controls.label: Have you determined whether you will use any available authentication services or systems to verify the authenticity of non-individual customer documents?
controls.detail-label: List any authentication services you use for non-individual verification (e.g., ASIC lookups, registry checks)
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must address whether you will use any available authentication services (such as ASIC company searches, land registry checks, or other government or third-party verification systems) to verify documents provided for non-individual customers. Document your decision on the use of authentication services in your AML/CTF program, as required under rule 4.9.5(5).

controls.label: Have you established a procedure to determine whether, and how, you will confirm KYC information about a non-individual customer by independently initiating contact with the customer?
controls.detail-label: Describe your process for independently verifying non-individual customer information through direct contact
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure must include a policy on whether you will conduct independent verification by directly contacting the non-individual customer to confirm their KYC information, and if so, under what circumstances and through what methods. Document this approach in your AML/CTF program, as required under rule 4.9.5(6).

groups.title: Process Confirmation
groups.description: Before answering the detailed compliance questions, confirm whether your organisation operates this business process.

groups.title: Document Verification Framework
groups.description: Establish whether your AML/CTF program provides for verification of KYC information using reliable and independent documentation as part of your identity verification process.

groups.title: Document Validity and Expiry Checks
groups.description: Ensure that all documents used for KYC verification are current and valid before acceptance.

groups.title: Document Requirements for Individuals
groups.description: Define which reliable and independent documents are required to verify the identity and residential information of individual customers.

groups.title: Document Copy Policy — Individuals
groups.description: Establish risk-based criteria for accepting copies of documents (such as certified copies or scans) when verifying individual customer information.

groups.title: Document Fraud Detection — Individuals
groups.description: Define the circumstances and procedures for detecting and investigating potentially forged, tampered, cancelled, or stolen documents from individual customers.

groups.title: Document Authentication Services — Individuals
groups.description: Determine whether you will use available authentication services or verification systems to confirm the authenticity of individual customer documents.

groups.title: Independent Contact Verification — Individuals
groups.description: Establish a procedure for independently verifying an individual customer's identity by directly contacting them.

groups.title: Document Verification Framework — Non-Individual Customers
groups.description: Establish whether your AML/CTF program provides for verification of KYC information about non-individual customers (companies, trusts, partnerships) using reliable and independent documentation.

groups.title: Document Quantity and Types — Non-Individual Customers
groups.description: Define what reliable and independent documents are required, and in what quantities, to verify information about non-individual customers.

groups.title: Document Contemporaneousness — Non-Individual Customers
groups.description: Establish risk-based criteria to assess whether documents are sufficiently current and relevant for verifying non-individual customer information.

groups.title: Document Copy Policy — Non-Individual Customers
groups.description: Establish risk-based criteria for accepting copies of documents when verifying non-individual customer information.

groups.title: Document Fraud Detection — Non-Individual Customers
groups.description: Define the circumstances and procedures for detecting and investigating potentially cancelled, forged, tampered, or stolen documents from non-individual customers.

groups.title: Document Authentication Services — Non-Individual Customers
groups.description: Determine whether you will use available authentication services (such as government registries or issuer verification systems) to confirm the authenticity of non-individual customer documents.

groups.title: Independent Contact Verification — Non-Individual Customers
groups.description: Establish a procedure for independently verifying a non-individual customer's information by directly contacting the customer.
